name: detect-dead-wiring
description: Find features that are implemented + tested but never reachable at runtime. Symptoms: a type's ::new()/.method() appears ONLY inside #[cfg(test)]; a CLI flag is defined on a struct the binary never parses (a separate Args); a computed value is stashed in a field then discarded by the aggregator. Grep for the constructor/flag across non-test code; if zero live call sites, it's dead-wired. RuView hit this 5+ times (#872 MQTT, #803 count, SemanticBus, semantic->MQTT routing, --semantic-zones-file). A green test suite does NOT prove reachability from main.

name: safety-by-construction-guards
description: When changing a safety-relevant threshold, count, or baseline, make the change provably one-directional so it cannot regress the proven behavior. Examples: person-count fix could only RAISE the count (never inflate the single-occupant case); the adaptive presence floor could only RISE above the proven 0.03 (never become more trigger-happy); a restored inactivity baseline is floored at the 30-min safety minimum. Encode the invariant as a regression test.

name: cut-flawed-design-under-review
description: When a reviewer surfaces a genuine DESIGN defect (not a style nit) mid-PR, drop the feature from that PR and ship the solid half, rather than patching a broken mechanism under time pressure. RuView dropped an adaptive presence threshold (model-bypass + can't-bootstrap-in-noise) and kept only the persistence half — the PR got cleaner and more honest. Re-attempt the dropped piece as a dedicated change with a correct design (and, for accuracy-bound features, field validation).

name: persist-learned-not-session-state
description: To survive restarts, persist ONLY long-lived learned values (an inactivity baseline, a resting-HR EMA), never session-relative FSM timers (idle_since, enter_since) which must reset on restart. Restore into a fresh FSM and FLOOR safety-critical values so a stale/corrupt store can't make a gate fire too eagerly. Distinguish NotFound (cold start, info) from corrupt/IO errors (warn) on load.

name: audit-before-claiming
description: Before asserting a capability works, trace the end-to-end data path and grep for todo!/unimplemented!/placeholder/#[cfg(test)]-only in the hot path. RuView's 'vital signs from a $9 ESP32' was half-false: WiFi heart-rate is scaffold (not linked into the server); credible vitals come from a $15 mmWave module whose firmware does the work. The valuable IP was in the sensor, not the narrative.

name: right-tool-wrong-scale
description: Evaluate a new dependency against the ACTUAL bottleneck and deployment scale, not its headline benchmark. turbovec's 16x memory win lands at 10M vectors; RuView's edge re-ID has a few hundred 128-dim vectors and is bottlenecked by CSI DSP, so adopting it would be premature optimization. Defer scale-tech to the scale phase; prefer the stack already integrated (ruvector in-process for the Rust runtime).

name: honest-accuracy-reporting
description: Never report a single in-room accuracy number. Always report held-out, cross-subject, AND cross-environment separately, each with a confidence interval (Wilson/bootstrap). RuView's ADR-150 found cross-environment presence collapses to ~10% without per-room calibration, and an earlier '100% presence' was retracted (it was a single-class recording). For events (bed_exit/fall) headline the missed-event rate and nuisance-alerts/night, not just precision.

name: reuse-existing-tap-not-new-path
description: When wiring a new consumer (MQTT/Matter/persistence) into a running system, route it through the broadcast/event tap that already exists rather than adding parallel plumbing. RuView's semantic->MQTT routing reused the same tokio::broadcast the WS bridge consumes; persistence hooked the existing tick task; zones reused the per-node node_features breakdown. Additive, low-risk, no new routes.

name: non-clinical-framing-for-regulatory-fit
description: For a privacy/health-adjacent edge product in Brazil: position as non-clinical 'ambient assistive' sensing to avoid ANVISA medical-device registration; treat fall/inactivity as assistive signals prompting a human check, not diagnoses. The no-camera/on-device/edge-only architecture is an LGPD asset (sensitive-data minimization). ANATEL homologation of the radio hardware is the real gate; field validation is what unlocks any defensible accuracy claim.
